title: Constrained Poetry
content:
Small Machines of Mercy  

The chipped blue cup still holds yesterday’s tea,  
a quiet testament to warmth that once passed through.  
The sleeping radio hums a lullaby for the dust  
that settles softly on forgotten edges.  

Rain fills the glove like a secret held tight—  
not poured out, but kept,  
waiting for a hand to remember how to receive.  

Each image is a small machine,  
moving parts of tenderness  
that never stop, even in stillness.

messages.content: You are being evaluated as a creative-writing model. Follow every visible structural and length constraint. Return only the finished creative work, without analysis, planning notes, hidden reasoning, or XML-style wrappers.

Write a free-verse poem. The first nonblank line must be the exact title 'Small Machines of Mercy'. After the title, write exactly 16 nonblank poem lines. Include the images of a chipped blue cup, a sleeping radio, and rain inside a glove. Do not use bullets or numbering. Do not use any of these words: eternity, forever, soul.
